excerpt.rendered: Robert Skrob explains the Connector Effect and how it makes BNI the single most effective growth strategy for business owners.

title.rendered: Episode 884: I Can’t Refer You

content.rendered:
Synopsis

If you’ve been a BNI member for a while and aren’t receiving referrals despite good weekly presentations and regular One-to-Ones, you may need to get creative.

If you’re in a profession like dentistry, where most of your chapter members already have a dentist, remind them that they can call on you if they have a dental emergency and can’t get an appointment with their regular dentist. A BNI member in India did this and ended up receiving a glowing testimonial from the member whose lost filling he replaced. The referrals started flooding in.

If you’re in a profession that the members of your chapter don’t know much about or have experience with, offer a free consultation or examination. The chiropractor in Dr. Misner’s original chapter did this and so impressed the first person who came in that referrals started to flood in. Recently Priscilla took the new chiropractor in her chapter up on such an offer and was so amazed at how good he was that she would send all the listeners to him if they lived close enough.

Have you converted skeptical or reluctant prospects with an offer like this? Let us know in the comments.

Complete Transcript of Episode 884

Priscilla:
Hello everybody, and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak recording studio in Berkeley, California, and I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan, how are you and where are you?

Ivan:
I am in Charlotte this week at BNI Global Headquarters. It’s always great to see headquarters. It has really grown in the last six months or a year, so it’s fun to be here.

Priscilla:
Oh, great. And so what is this podcast about?

Ivan:
Well, it’s based on an email that I received from a dentist in India, and I’m gonna read his email to you.

Priscilla:
Okay.

Ivan:
And then I’m gonna tell you a story. He said, “It’s great connecting with you. I’m a dentist. I joined BNI for exposure and visibility. I do get noticed by our BNI members a lot. I’ve got many really good 30-second presentations.” (He’s in a large chapter, so they do 30-second Weekly Presentations.) He says, “But still, as a dentist, I can’t compete with the other members in the race for referrals. Whatever I do to generate referrals, I’m not getting anything compared to other members.” And so he’s asking for help. He says, “I’m lagging somewhere. I want to learn. What can I do?”

So there’s a couple things. First of all, it’s really important to remember that you can’t compare all professions. You know, we oftentimes talk about the average number of referrals in a [BNI] Chapter, the average amount of Thank You for Closed Business in a Chapter. An average is a valuable number to understand. But also remember that if your head is in the oven and your feet are in the freezer, on average, you should be okay.

Priscilla: (laughing)

Ivan:
And you’re not. So there’s a range, and the professions really differ. And certainly some professions, you know, a referral is worth a lot more. A referral for a dentist could be worth a lot more than, say, a referral to a florist. So do remember that the number of referrals alone is not the factor. The value of the referrals is an important factor.

Now that aside, let’s talk about the dentist issue. Because when people are going to see a dentist and they’re okay with their dentist, they don’t even have to be totally crazy about their dentist, but if they’re okay, they’re hesitant to leave their dentist.

So years ago, in one of the first four or five chapters of BNI, we had a dentist who joined, and he’d been a member — he was a member for 20 or 25 years before he retired–and I remember talking to him and he said, “You know, there are people who’ve come up to me in the chapter, and they said, ‘Well, I’m seeing a dentist. And, you know, I’ve seen him a long time, and I’m not sure I can switch over. It’s not that easy for me to switch over.'” And he asked me, “What do I do?”

And I said, “Well, I can understand that. And so what you want to do is just tell the chapter. I know some of you already have a dentist. I get it, but at some point you may have an opportunity to refer me to someone else, or you may be in a situation where your dentist isn’t available, and I promise you, I will bring you in ASAP [as soon as possible] if your dentist isn’t available for some problem.” And I said, “Just keep saying that.”

And sure enough, within six months, there was a BNI member who had a filling that fell out, [the member] called his dentist. His dentist was on vacation. They were going to refer him to another dentist at the office. And he said, “Well, let me call you back.”

And he thought, ‘Why should I go see another dentist when I have this dentist in my chapter? And this is exactly what he’d been talking about, if I can’t see my dentist, just call him.’ So he said, “I called him.” I spoke to this member, and he said, “I called him, and boom, the dentist got me in that day. He found a spot, brought me in.” He said, “I have never had the kind of welcome in a dentist’s office that I had there. He showed me around his offices, he introduced me to his staff. He clearly wanted me to understand the kind of operation that he ran, and that if I were to refer people, the kind of care that he would give them.” And then, and this was the great thing, he said, “I liked my dentist, but our member dentist gave the closest thing to a completely painless shot I have ever had in my entire life. It was like no big deal.”

Priscilla: (chuckling)

Ivan:
“And he, you know, did the filling. We talked about what else might need to be done down the road.” And he said, “That day I switched dentists, and I went to the BNI dentist from then on.” He said, “I’m committed to going to the BNI dentist from now on.” And I talked to the dentist about it, and he said, “Yeah, it was amazing how that worked.” But he said, “That’s not the great thing.” I said, “Well, what’s the great thing?”

He said, “At the next meeting, the member stood up and said, ‘Oh, you’re all crazy if you don’t go to our dentist.’ He said, ‘I was really hesitant, because I’d been seeing a dentist for a long time, but I had this filling fall out, so I figured, what the heck, I’ll see our dentist here, and I went to see him.” And he said, “He gives the closest thing to a painless shot I’ve ever had. It was truly amazing. He’s a fantastic dentist, I’m telling you, go see his office. He’ll walk you around. He’ll show you, even if it’s just a cleaning. Go see his office, so you can become familiar with what he does.”

From that day on, he started getting a boatload of referrals — members at first going in to check him out, but then other people that the members knew after that, and he ended up being a member, Priscilla, for almost 25 years. And he stuck it out until that first member referred him, or went to him actually, and then did a testimonial. This is why, this is the power of testimonials. This is why testimonials are so incredibly important.

Priscilla:
Yeah. Well, I have a story like that.

Ivan:
Tell me.

Priscilla: 
I’ve been under chiropractic care since I was 26 years old, and I hurt my back doing construction, and my chiropractor retired, and we had another chiropractor in my group, and then she left. And I was so hesitant to try the new chiropractor in my group, but, finally, I decided to do it. I was tired of driving really far away to use the other chiropractor, right?

He is so talented. I was absolutely shocked. It’s the same kind of thing I had my first adjustment, and afterwards, I was like, painless. It was like, what happened? So I’ve been just so excited to tell everybody, and he’s ending up taking care of most of our chapter. So it’s the same kind of thing. I was like, I was just shocked.

Ivan:
You know, [BNI] testimonials came about because of chiropractors. I think I’ve said this in a podcast, but I’m not sure. I remember vividly, Dr. Rubin, chiropractor in the Founding Chapter of BNI, he came to me and he said, “I’m not getting any referrals.” And you got to think back, this is 1985 and chiropractors in 1985 — a lot of people didn’t think they were real medical professionals. And of course, they are. And my late wife was a chiropractic assistant, and I met her while she was a chiropractic assistant. So I know how valuable chiropractic is, but certainly in the mid ’80s, a lot of people didn’t.

And he said, “Nobody is even talking to me about chiropractic, which makes me think that nobody’s used a chiropractor.” And so I said to him, “Why don’t you do a free initial consultation, so that people can use you and see what it’s like?” And so he did, and he included an X-ray, and that was back in the day where it wasn’t digital, you know, he had to have a processing center in his office where he could process the X-rays.

Priscilla:
Ahh, right.

Ivan:
Yeah, he had and he did. He had it all right there. So, you know, someone said, “Okay, what the heck? You know, I’ll come. It’s a free consultation, what the heck.” He came in, he got a tour, just like the dentist did. He gave a tour of the offices, showed everything that he did, and he adjusted him. And he said, “Come on back in two or three days.” He adjusted him again. And this member came in, and he said to the members, he stood up, and this was back when I first started running BNI. I was like, if you had a referral, give it and if you didn’t, pass. That was the process. If you have a referral, give it; if you don’t, pass. Well, this member stood up. He said, “Well, I don’t have referral, but I don’t want to pass, Ivan.” I said, “Okay, what do you want to do?” He said, “I want to say something.” I said, “Okay, what do you want to say?”

He said, “Well, I want to talk about Dr Rubin. He said, “I just went to see him. I want to talk about it.” I said, “Okay.” And so he just said in glowing terms about how he’d always had this lower back problem, and after just a few adjustments, his lower back didn’t hurt nearly as much. And then he told the chapter, “You’re all crazy if you don’t take him up on this offer.” And I look around the room, and this is back when we all had [paper] referral slips, and all kinds of people are filling out referral slips for the chiropractor. And that’s when it hit me that a testimonial can be even more powerful than a referral, because the testimonial can get multiple referrals.

Priscilla:
Right.

Ivan:
And that’s what happened with this dentist that I told the story about, and that’s certainly what happened with the chiropractor that I told the story about. So for the dentist who sent me that message from email, get somebody to use you once and then have them talk about their experience, and that’s a great way to get more referrals. I’m glad you shared your story, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Yeah, thank you.

Ivan:
That’s all I have for today.

Priscilla:
Okay, well, thank you for the great information. I think that’s a really special podcast.

This podcast is sponsored by www.misneraudioprograms.com. These audio programs will provide you with the tools and the inspiration to powerfully enhance your BNI experience. So check out the great material available to you at www.misneraudioprograms.com and then use the promo code IVAN50 for 50% off of everything [this code is good for a limited time]. All of the proceeds go to the BNI Foundation.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

excerpt.rendered: Get creative with your offerings if you aren’t getting referrals. Special offers can produce fantastic testimonials and multiple referrals.

title.rendered: Episode 883: Fear Forward

content.rendered:
Synopsis

Dr. Oudi Abouchacra, co-author of Work Your Network with the Four Cs, explains his F.E.A.R Forward method to take a quantum leap toward your vision for your business and your life.

  1. Inventory your conscious and unconscious fears. Recognize that we fear not the mystery of tomorrow but the history of yesterday.
  2. Establish the source of those fears: what happened in the past that you’re afraid will happen again?
  3. Rewrite your story. Change the narrative and leave your fear behind you.

Links

  • Listen to Episode 680, A Beacon of Hope in a Sea of Fear
  • Get your copy of Work Your Network with the Four Cs.
  • Get your copy of Fear Forward: A 4-Step method to free yourself from stress, struggles & fears (UAE) (Amazon).
  • Visit Dr. Oudi’s website, Inspired Results.
  • Have you rewritten your story? Apply to be in the Inspired movie.

Complete Transcript of Episode 883

Priscilla:
Hello everybody, and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak recording studio in Berkeley, California, and I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan, how are you and where are you?

Ivan:
I actually have some grass growing under my feet today, and I’m home in Austin, Texas, so…

Priscilla:
Oh, great.

Ivan:
It’s good to be in one place. I have a good friend on as a guest today. I’ve known him for a number of years. Dr. Oudi is the founder of Inspired Results, which is a global performance coaching training company in Abu Dhabi, and he’s got two decades of experience as a chiropractor, coach and speaker. He’s a renowned performance expert. He and I have spoken on stage together on a couple of occasions. He was formerly an E.D. [Executive Director] for BNI Abu Dhabi, and he is my co-author on a book that I have talked about several times here on BNI podcast, Work Your Network With the 4Cs, and I have him here on my podcast today. Dr. Oudi, welcome.

Dr. Oudi:
Thank you. Thank you for having me. My pleasure.

Ivan:
So you have an interesting topic, Fear Forward. Now, the whole topic was Fear Forward to live an inspired professional life. Talk to me about that. How do you move forward in business and face your fears?

Dr. Oudi:
Yeah, well, you know, first of all, let’s review what most people do. Most people actually avoid even addressing their fears, and they face every other thing on their to do list, every goal they have and maybe their bucket list, but they don’t actually face the thing that could probably leverage their life and get them to the next level, which is their fears. 

Ivan:
Yeah, why do you think that is?

Dr. Oudi:
Well, I think it’s because we’ve been taught that the fundamental human fear is fear the unknown, and I believe it’s totally antiquated and is it’s totally inaccurate. It’s really what’s holding people back.

Ivan:
So what happens then, when you break free from that chains of what are holding you back?

Dr. Oudi:
Well, I think what happens is you’ll take a quantum leap forward, and you’ll start to approach your vision. But again, it starts with changing the model that’s operating in our minds. We think that we’re afraid of the unknown, but you can’t actually fear the unknown.

Ivan:
Yeah, I know a lot of people in BNI that get caught up in some fear or another about whether they’re good enough or whether they are, you know, bringing enough referrals, or doing a good job for people, and they get caught up in those fears. And they get, I came up with this phrase a number of years ago, you either get frozen by fear or focused by fear, and when you get focused by fear, you have a lot more success than when you get frozen by fear. So I think you have some steps to help people get out of fear. What are those, I think, three steps?

Dr. Oudi:
Yes well, step one is we gotta break the existing paradigm in people’s minds, because that’s really what’s holding them back. So if we’re not afraid of the unknown, what I’m proposing is that we’re afraid of the known, because you can’t actually generate an emotion without some substance, some information, the definition or content in mind. And so fear of the unknown is completely false, which means we fear the known, and the known is our past. So what we’re really afraid of is actually running into yesterday, tomorrow.

Ivan:
Hmmm. There is… I wonder if I can remember the phrase — today is the day we were afraid of yesterday, or something like that. Today is the tomorrow we were afraid of yesterday.

Dr. Oudi:
Yeah, yeah. But that’s the idea — we think we’re afraid of the mystery of tomorrow when we’re really afraid of the mystery of yesterday.

Ivan:
Yeah.

Dr. Oudi:
And if I can share with you a really short story to kind of drive that point home.

Ivan:
Yeah, absolutely.

Dr. Oudi:
So this entrepreneur came to see me with a health complaint. She had neck pain and a blocked ear. She’d been all around the world and wasn’t able to find a solution. And what I said to her is, let’s just take a minute and look at what’s going on in the mind. What are you most afraid of? And she said, “I’m afraid of not being able to have a child.” And this was not only, you know, impacting her health, but it was impacting her business as well.

So I said to her, “Maybe you don’t want to have a child.” She’s like, “No, no, I I really do. We’ve tried IVF, I even carried and had failed pregnancies.” And I said, “Look, I believe you, but I believe maybe your mind is working against you. And what I said to her was, “If right now I snapped my fingers and you would have a child in this moment, what would be your greatest fear?” She took a deep breath and she said, “Oh, my goodness, I’m afraid of turning out like my mother.”

Ivan:
Mmmm.

Dr. Oudi:
Right? So when we cleared her fear–that’s probably a common one–when we cleared her fear, all her symptoms dropped off. I was talking to a chiropractor friend, and she said, Oh yeah, it’s amazing that you didn’t adjust her. You didn’t adjust her physically, just adjusted her thinking. And I say, I adjusted her story, and then all of a sudden, her symptoms dropped off. And I said, Yeah.

She said, that’s amazing. But did she get pregnant? And I was like, I’m not sure. She’s like, what’s wrong with you? That’s the most important part of the story.

And I said, well, listen, I’d lost touch with her, you know. And what was interesting is, it was a year and a day after I coached her, and when I did coach her, by the way, all her symptoms dropped off. But she also connected with her mom. She said that they were scheduled to see each other again, and she put the past behind her, because it was an event, of course, that she didn’t to relive and then guess what happened?

It was amazing is that day, a year and a day later, I was just kind of doodling with my pencil on the paper and playing with the acronym fear, and I get a message from her, and it wasn’t a message saying that she got pregnant, because that would be next level. Do you agree?

Ivan:
Yeah, absolutely.

Dr. Oudi:
Well, it was even better than that. She sent me a picture of her new baby.

Ivan:
Oh, amazing!

Dr. Oudi:
Yeah, I can tell you that as business professionals, we have the same competing fears, right? She had a fear of not having a child, and she had a fear of having a child. And in business, we have a fear of succeeding, and we have a fear of failing. So failure and success are oftentimes competing fears that are pulling us apart.

Ivan:
Yeah, that’s powerful. That’s really powerful. Now, did you throw in your step two in there, which is, I think about, when did I experience something similar? Is that something different?

Dr. Oudi:
Yes, yeah. So step one is realize that what we’re afraid of is not the mystery of tomorrow, it’s the history of yesterday. In that first step as well, you can do an inventory and list your fears. I have a fear of my business failing. I have a fear of my business succeeding. I have a fear of public speaking, etc. List your fears. Okay, so that’s step one.

Then step two, you want to establish the source. Where does this fear come from? When did I experience something similar? So when I ask business professionals, you know you’re afraid of your business failing. Okay, so if you’re afraid of your business failing, you must have experienced it directly, you had a failed business before, or indirectly, you witnessed someone, or watched or heard about someone that failed in business, and that’s what you’re afraid of repeating. That’s the second step.

The third step is rewriting the story. So as a chiropractor, for many years, I was moving bones around and I was adjusting bones, but what I’m doing now is I’m adjusting stories. I’m helping people realize that they are the producers of their own movie. They’re the directors of their own movie. You can change the narrative, you can change the story, and then fear just drops off, and you’re no longer one step behind fear.

Ivan:
So is there anything else on those three steps that you want to talk about? Because you had a great segue about rewriting your story, that I want to jump into. But anything else on those three steps?

Dr. Oudi:
Just that sometimes it’s not obvious, like you think. You know, when I coach people, when they’re afraid of public speaking, I remember speaking to one gal in particular, and she said, “Well, how can I be afraid of history repeating itself, when, frankly, there is no history. I’ve never spoken on a grand stage before.” And what I say is, you know, if you’re looking for the exact same scenario, you’re not going to find it, but you need to look for critical elements. And this one particular gal that I’m thinking of, she actually spoke up in English in [her] English class, and she had broken English, and they laughed her. So that’s what she was afraid of.

Ivan:
That makes sense. You’re talking about rewriting your story and you’re producing a documentary based on some what we discussed today. You want to talk about that briefly?

Dr. Oudi:
Yeah, absolutely. So the idea is, I really want people to understand that what’s holding us back, as Napoleon Hill said, is fear, and fear, he said, is the most costly human emotion. So that’s number one, realize that fear sits at the center of every problem, and if it sits at the center of every problem, it stands tall at the center of every solution, if we know how to leverage it. And then I want to, of course, debunk this idea that what we’re afraid of is the unknown.

We’re afraid of the known, which empowers us because with fear the unknown is disempowering because you can’t do anything about it, right? You’re literally… fear is one step ahead of you. You got to find out what you’re afraid of when you step onto the stage. And so it’s really a disempowering concept. And then what I want people to understand is what can happen when you break free, right? When you break free from those chains that are holding you back, you can transform your business. It can transform your health, you can bring another human being into this world. It can really take your life to the next level.

Ivan:
And as I understand, you’ve got in the movie, Brian Tracy, John Demartini, have come on board. I believe you’re on board, and somebody else is on board that you had a conversation with.

Dr. Oudi:
Yeah, that would be Ivan Misner from BNI.

Ivan:
Yes. I just agreed to be part of the movie as well. And so if someone’s interested in more information on the movie, they can email you at info@droudi.com. That’s D, R, o, u, d, i.com, and the movie website is inspired.movie. Is that right?

Dr. Oudi:
Exactly. That’s perfect. Ivan, yes, thank you.

Ivan:
Anything else before we hand it back to Priscilla?

Dr. Oudi:
Yeah, I want listeners to just understand one thing. If you want to focus on business, then just take a piece of paper, or put it in your computer or something, and just document all the conscious fears that you have, all the things you know you’re afraid of — I’m afraid of public speaking, I’m afraid of criticism, I’m afraid of whatever. And also realize that in this inventory of fears, you can also list unconscious fears. These are fears that are operating under a different title, so you might call it stress or struggle or obstacle or issue or challenge. Those are, for me, code for fear. So just make a list, because that’s where it starts.

Ivan:
We’ll be working on this movie together to hopefully get there, and as the movie progresses, I’ll make sure and put it out on this podcast.

Dr. Oudi:
Amazing. Ivan, thank you so much.

Ivan:
Dr. Oudi, thank you so much for being on BNI Podcast, and thanks for being a co-author on Work Your Network With the 4Cs. Priscilla, back to you.

Priscilla:
Okay, great. Was that Inspired.movie.com?

Ivan:
No. It’s Inspired.movie.

Priscilla:
And that’s it? You just put that in? www.Inspired.movie?

Ivan:
Yeah, I just tried it. It’s inspired.movie. You don’t even have to put the www.

Priscilla:
Ah, okay, okay, great. All right, thank you. It sounds very interesting. I’m gonna go look it up.

Ivan:
Good.

Priscilla:
Okay, well, thanks for the great information from both of you.

This podcast is sponsored by www.misneraudioprograms.com. These audio programs will provide you with the tools and the inspiration to powerfully enhance your BNI experience. So check out the great material available to you at www.misneraudioprograms.com and then use the promo code IVAN50 for 50% off of everything [this code is good for a limited time]. All of the proceeds go to the BNI Foundation.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

title.rendered: Episode 882: Having a BNI Guide

content.rendered:
Synopsis

Networking is like whitewater rafting: you need an expert guide to succeed. Second-generation BNI member Kamryn Reynolds explains the difference having a BNI guide makes in a new member’s success–and what a difference acting as a BNI guide makes in the whole chapter’s success.

Many new BNI members don’t even know what they don’t know about networking. If you notice a new member struggling, talk to them. Show them how to make Givers Gain and BNI work for them. Take them to trainings. Help them to gain Visibility and Credibility. Take them to other chapters. Introduce them to key people that would be good for them in their business.

Links

  • Visit Kamryn’s website, Articulate Advantage.
  • See Ashley Misner’s art.

Complete Transcript of Episode 882

Priscilla:
Hello everybody, and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak recording studio in Berkeley, California, and I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan, how are you and where are you?

Ivan:  
I am doing great, and I’m headed to L.A. tomorrow on a personal trip. My eldest daughter is having an art show. She just had some health issues and did a lot of paintings, more than a dozen paintings, and is going to be in a gallery. And the hospital, City of Hope, is actually supporting the gallery showing. So I’m going to be there tomorrow and be there to support her for her art show.

Priscilla:
Oh, that’s great. Didn’t she just have a baby, also?

Ivan:  
She did, a year and a half ago, yeah.

Priscilla:
Yeah.

Ivan:  
And her website is ashleymisner.com for anybody that wants to see some.

Priscilla:
Oh, yeah.

Ivan:  
I have a guest today, and his name is Kamryn Reynolds. Kamryn has been immersed in BNI since he was 14 years old. He has been helping his father, Spencer Reynolds, who is the Executive Director of BNI Tampa [Florida, USA], launch his first chapter. Today, Kamryn plays a number of roles, including serving as a successful Launch Specialist recently leading a Hall of Fame Launch with 36 members at BNI Champions. He’s also founder of the Articulate Advantage, a communication coaching firm, which is all about empowering professionals to master their voice, enhance their public speaking, and leadership skills. Kamryn believes that when you master your voice, you amplify your impact. Kamryn Reynolds, welcome to BNI Podcast. It’s great to have you on.

Kamryn:  
Thank you, Ivan, it’s good to be on.

Ivan:  
It’s fun to do these podcasts. Now, our topic today is about having a BNI guide, and you had such an interesting analogy that you wanted to use, I thought it would make a great podcast. So talk about what you mean by a guide, because it has a very specific meaning to you. 

Kamryn:  
Absolutely. So, this whole subject is the convergence of two experiences I’ve had recently. One, it was a once-in-a-lifetime experience that my family, we got to go on. And it was in the Grand Canyon [Arizona, USA]. We went on this nine-day whitewater rafting trip. Now, in the Grand Canyon, there’s 12-foot rapids, you know, there’s 100-degree air temperature, and then you get smacked in the face with 50-degree water. So it’s all these crazy things with huge rocks you might hit into. And our river guide, his name was Scotty Miller. Now he’s a good friend of my father, Spencer Reynolds, from back when he was in BNI originally, 17 years ago, when my father was a mortgage broker and Scotty, he was the auto glass guy.

Now, Scotty, he’s a third-generation river guide. This wasn’t his first run down the Grand Canyon, and his job is to make sure that we’re having fun, but most importantly, that we stay alive. You know, this is a very possibly dangerous thing to be doing, and they make that very clear in the orientation with, you know, the state park people that interview you. And so this is where the idea of a guide really came in, because there’s no chance whatsoever that me or anyone in my family could have done this, could have navigated the river, could have navigated the canyon, and done it safely without this guide.

Ivan:  
So make that connection to BNI. How does that connect or fit with BNI?

Kamryn:  
So earlier this year, I met one… of what is now one of my best friends. His name is Joey Kenrick. He is amazing. He’s, you know, a fellow 21-year old, and he started in BNI when he was 19. He will tell you, he’ll be the first one to tell you that he was a horrible member. Six months in, he had only received $2,000 of Thank You for Closed Business. And he hadn’t really given anything. He’s, what he says, is a taker. He came into the room and he just wanted to take, take, take. And so, coming from this point, his chapter president, Jeremiah Williams, another good friend, you know, he saw that this new member was six months in, and so he got with Joey, and he said, “What are you doing, man?” And you know, Joey didn’t really know what he was doing.

And Jeremiah said one thing that changed the course of Joey’s BNI career, his space in BNI. He told Joey, “You have no credibility.” And that changed Joey’s look on everything, because he is a man of his word. He is the most credible person I know. If he said he’s going to do something, he’s going to do it. And so when Jeremiah told him that, that flipped the world up on its head for Joey. And so he then asked Jeremiah, “Okay, well, what do I need to do then?” So Jeremiah said, “Let me show you.”

Ivan:  
Can I just add something to it here? I have said for years, it’s not your confidence that counts, it’s somebody else’s confidence in your ability to provide a quality product or service. And I think that’s what you’re talking a little bit about here, is that he may have been very confident in his ability, but he hadn’t, he hadn’t established that credibility with the other members yet, and that’s what needed to be done. And this is a message that is applicable to so many BNI members, and I’m glad you’re bringing it up. So sorry, I cut you off. Keep going.

Kamryn:  
Absolutely, that’s so true. And so Jeremiah became this guide for Joey. Jeremiah, he was, you know, his chapter president. He was not the mentor. He was not directly responsible for him in any way, but he took him under his wing. He helped him, showed him how to do the things. He took him to advanced trainings with him. He took him to other chapters to meet key people that would be good for him in his business. He showed him how to use Givers gain to support him and support their chapter as a whole.

Now to really show the transition in Joey’s life — I actually met him a couple months into this process, when he was building up his philosophy in Givers Gain, and he was truly becoming a giver. Joey today, he’s actually a Director Consultant in our region, and he does amazing — visiting many chapters all around the Tampa Bay area, and he’s given well into the five figures in his chapter and the chapters surrounding him, and he’s received into the six figures. He’s become a very productive BNI member, and truly has the heart of a giver, but he didn’t know how to do it at first. It’s who he’s always been, but he just didn’t know how, until he had that guide help him.

Ivan:  
We don’t teach this in colleges and universities, and so we come into a BNI chapter or any networking group, and we’re there to just get business. And we don’t recognize what we have to do in order to not only get more business, but to give business so we can get more business. And that’s kind of what you’re describing here, that this person who took him under his wing was his guide.

Kamryn:  
Absolutely.

Ivan:  
Yeah, go ahead.

Kamryn:  
Yeah. So this concept with a guide is, you know, as you said, we don’t know what we’re doing. We’re not born networkers. You know, I’m currently a college student, and there’s no classes on networking or giving or how to support those around you. And so I believe now that’s a big part. And a lot of new BNI members don’t even know what they don’t know. And so it’s important for members, you know, they could be the president, they could be a membership committee member, they could be a visitor host, they could be any member of the chapter — just to care about a member that’s struggling and saying, “Hey, I know this is not natural for some people. I know you’ve never been taught how to do this. Let me help you.” So that’s the biggest thing, I believe, is just let me help you. Let me show you how, and then care about them enough to follow through with that and really do it.

Ivan:  
And the beauty of that, and you know, something I’ve said for years, is that we all have people in our story, but the real measure of a life is whose story are we in. And so this member is in Joey’s story, and that’s so important, because look at the amount of business that he’s been able to bring into the chapter. So that effort by the member to guide Joey has resulted in the chapter generating a whole lot more business. It’s a win, win situation.

Kamryn:  
Absolutely. And on top of that, he sponsored many, many more people into his chapter, and all around him, he’s become a very productive member that he wouldn’t have had the chance if he didn’t have this guide, Jeremiah, to help him do that.

Ivan:  
So what’s the difference, just to wrap up here, what’s the difference between this concept of being a guide versus being a mentor in the mentor program?

Kamryn:  
I think when we think of mentor in BNI, we think of the 10 mentors that you have a One-to-One with at the beginning of your membership. You get through the Passport to Success Program, and you learn each of the key things that you need as a new member. And I think that’s what we think of when we think of mentor. We don’t really think of this guide that isn’t an official role. It’s something that is just, you know, I care about you enough to help you. And I think that’s the real difference of it is it’s not a dedicated role. It’s more of let me help you type of thing.

Ivan:  
Well, I think the concept of a guide is a brilliant one. I also think that the guide can be a mentor. And, you know, using the mentor program to be a guide goes hand-in-hand with one another, in my opinion.

Kamryn:  
Of course.

Ivan:  
So Kamryn, any last thoughts before we wrap up?

Kamryn:  
You know, I just think if you have that member in your chapter, or even in the neighboring chapter, that you see just has the heart of a giver and truly wants to be able to help, but just doesn’t know how, you can care enough to help them.

Ivan:  
Yeah, give them a little coaching and support.

Kamryn:  
Yeah, absolutely.

Ivan:  
Well, thank you so much for being on BNI Podcast today. I really appreciate your time, and you had a great message. Thank you.

Kamryn:  
Thank you, Ivan.

Ivan:  
Over to you, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Okay, that was some great information. I have to say that I’ve been down the Grand Canyon and a in a rafting trip also, and it is super exciting.

This podcast is sponsored by www.misneraudioprograms.com. These audio programs will provide you with the tools and the inspiration to powerfully enhance your BNI experience. So check out the great material available to you at www.misneraudioprograms.com and then use the promo code IVAN50 for 50% off of everything [this code is good for a limited time]. All of the proceeds go to the BNI Foundation.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

title.rendered: Episode 881: The Customer Care Checklist

content.rendered:
Synopsis

Learn how to use the Customer Care Checklist to generate referrals. Julian Lewis, co-author of Dr. Misner’s latest book, Infinite Legacy, shares the story of a BNI member who generated 69 referrals in two months using this checklist.

The Customer Care Checklist is a set of questions to ask your clients to help introduce them to people who can make a positive impact on their businesses and their lives.

Each member’s checklist will be different, because your questions have to complement your business and to be relevant for your clients. There’s a sample below to help you brainstorm with your Power Team.

If there is something you know is in your client’s interest, but no one in your chapter has that expertise, put the question on your checklist anyway and find a BNI member in another chapter you can refer them to.

Links

  • Get your copy of Infinite Giving.
  • Get your copy of Infinite Legacy.
  • Visit Julian’s website.

Download the Customer Care Checklist Sample

Get the PDF
Get the Word Doc

Complete Transcript of Episode 881

Priscilla:
Hello everybody, and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak recording studio in Berkeley, California, and I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan, how are you and where are you?

Ivan:
I just got back from Miami. I did a membership drive cruise for BNI United States. We had well over 200 people there, and great cruise event, and I spoke in Miami, and now I’m back home.

Priscilla:
Okay, was that a fun trip?

Ivan:
Yes, it’s always great to see a lot of members. I’m not a big fan of cruising, but I am a big fan of [BNI] members, so it’s always a lot of fun. I spent a lot of time with members at dinner and in the bar, (I know what a shock) a few other places.

Priscilla:
Okay, great.

Ivan:
So we have with us today a good friend of mine, Julian Lewis. Julian is a portfolio entrepreneur. In 1998 he founded his first business, an IT support company, and he’s an award winning BNI Executive Director based in the United Kingdom, and a Founding Partner of the coaching company, Integrus Global. He’s co-author of the Amazon best-selling book, Infinite Giving, along with me and Greg Davies. And I’m really, really proud to say that he’s also a co-author in my latest book, Infinite Legacy. And that book just recently came out; we’ll make sure and have a link in this podcast for the Infinite Legacy book.

You know, we’re about to celebrate BNI’s 40th anniversary, and so I thought this was the perfect book to do this year before our 40th. Before I bring Julian on, Julian’s core belief is the power of collaboration for personal and business success, and he envisions a world where everyone can achieve success simultaneously. Julian, welcome to the BNI Podcast.

Julian:
Oh, it’s wonderful to be here, Ivan, as always; wonderful to talk to you. And thank you for mentioning the books, both of which I’m very proud of. [Infinite] Legacy is a fantastic book, so really proud of it.

Ivan:
Yeah, I have a good friend who just finished about half of it and said they were really, really enjoying it. So I love the opening for the book. It’s a tremendous opening and well, I’m going to do a podcast or two on the book coming up, but today’s topic is about the Customer Care Checklist, and you have a story about a BNI member that you’d like to share, and we’d all love to hear it.

Julian:
Yes, absolutely. So Customer Care Checklist is a way of generating referrals, and you say often enough Ivan that we shouldn’t get distracted by shiny, bright, shiny objects sometimes. So before I approached you about doing the podcast, I was very keen that we got some kind of proof behind this process. And one of my members in Gloucester, Vicki Summersby, she achieved using the customer care checklist I’m going to explain in a minute, 69 referrals in two months. Which you know, across eight meetings, is 8.6 referrals to every meeting using the customer care checklist. So it is a really great tool when used correctly.

Ivan:
So tell me more about it. How did it work? How did she come up with it? Or you come up with it?

Julian:
Yes, I came up with it. And BNI’s mission, as you know, is helping people increase their business and using the structured marketing program, referral marketing. And back in the day, we used to give people lots of different tools, and it struck me that some of these needed modernizing slightly. And especially the tools that allow us to be creators of referrals, you know, the kind of top level people that are out there finding referrals, not just reacting to them or reacting to what people say.

So the customer care checklist is a way of us doing that. It’s a way of us looking after our clients. Because in BNI, you know, we like to invite people we care about to meet people we care about. We do that in chapters and as owners of businesses. We care about our clients. We certainly care about their success. And we also know in BNI that we’re surrounded by people that can make a very positive impact on people’s businesses and people’s lives. So the customer care checklist is a way of different members coming up with a different set of questions that would generate referrals.

Ivan:
So the checklist isn’t the same for everybody. It’s a little bit different for each person. So give us some hints and tips on ways someone, a BNI member, can produce their own customer care checklist.

Julian:
Yeah, absolutely. So there’s some real key things about the checklist, because I said about it generating referrals for your chapter. One of the things we need to guard against is not literally just going around the chapter and finding a question for everybody in our chapter, because some questions just won’t make sense for us as a supplier to our customers. So the questions must complement our business.

As an example, if I was selling insurance, I can quite easily introduce someone to someone that maybe does a tax planning for somebody. That’s a very obvious kind of link. I don’t do tax planning myself, but I know that my clients should have that service. But if I was introducing them to say, a plumber, in my customer care checklist, that would be a little bit weird. So everyone has to have their own version. And the tip number one is questions that complement your business. The other tip I talk about is questions that look out for your clients’ interest. They absolutely have to be in the interest of your client.

And this is another tip for BNI members. If there is something you know as a supplier that is in the interest of your client, but you don’t actually have anybody in your chapter that is covering that expertise, still put it on your checklist, and then you can find someone else within BNI, hopefully, but certainly within your network, that can cover that area. Because this does have to be about being authentic that you’re looking out for your clients. And I think we’re going to put up an example on the podcast for people to download. And it’s got about ten questions on one side, ten on the other. But the number of questions you ask has to be right for you in your business. So just like you said there, Ivan, it’s going to be different for different people.

Ivan:
And do we know what business, I haven’t seen the checklist that you’re gonna give me for us to post, what profession is the checklist that we’re gonna put up here?

Julian:
Okay, so the checklist is… I use my experience from a coaching point of view…

Ivan:
Okay.

Julian:
But lots of questions where you’re within your Power Team or your contact sphere, you can work on this in Power Team meetings to swap questions, and lots of them are the same within Power Teams anyway.

Ivan:
Okay, so the checklist that we’re going to post is a great example, but you don’t necessarily use that exact checklist in your own business.

Julian:
Absolutely not. It has to be right for you and your business. Possibly also, there will be some differences around the world, different countries, and different variations on what we call things and what other people call things. So it’s kind of, use it as an example, use it as inspiration.

Ivan:
Yeah, actually, I see it, you did send it. I’ve got it right here. And you’ve got questions like, Do you have a shareholder or partnership agreement? Do you have a suitable commercial insurance? Do you engage in proactive tax planning, pension planning? Yeah, these are good questions to ask. It’s a great checklist.

Julian:
Yeah, absolutely. And you know, there’s various ways we can create this, because it looks a bit daunting when you first see it. But you can ask the members of your chapter, they’re the experts in the particular topic, “So if I was going to ask a question to introduce you, what would I ask?”

Ivan:
Yeah.

Julian:
I also know that sometimes when you do that with people, they’re like, “Oh yeah, I’ll get back to you, I’ll get it to you soon.” and they don’t actually get it to you.

Ivan:
Yeah.

Julian:
So you can actually use AI to create the questions and supporting material, and then go to the expert and say, “Look, if I said this, is it correct?”

Ivan:
That’s a great idea. I’ve found AI to be very effective for doing surveys.

Julian:
Yeah. It does sometimes make mistakes, right?

Ivan:
Yeah, it does.

Julian:
So we have to just check in with the expert. And also, we want to make sure that what we’re getting at for their business, is correct.

Ivan:
Yes. So you have three ways that the checklist can be used based on your recommendations. What are those three ways?

Julian:
Yeah. So the most productive way, is face-to-face with a client. So you might be at a review meeting or even actually at a prospecting meeting, because that’s the second way you can use it is to make yourself stand out from the competition. Because if you’re doing this, you’re introducing yourself, you say, “This is my service. And on top of that, we care about our clients so much that here’s our Customer Care Checklist. If you come on board for us as a client, we can go through that. In actual fact, why don’t you just keep that? Because whether you use us or not, that’s it.”

And I bet your competition are not doing that, and then they’re not demonstrating how much they care about their clients. And the very last one quickly, is you can use it as promotional stuff. This is all blogs that you can use online to create referrals for your fellow members.

Ivan:
This is really good stuff, and you have a member who gave, based on using this checklist, gave 69 referrals within a period of just two months.

Julian:
Yep, using it during client reviews. She actually emailed it out, which is less effective than face-to-face, as you can understand, but still, the results speak for themselves. [It’s] pretty effective nonetheless.

Ivan:
Yeah, absolutely. What a great suggestion, and thank you for sharing this today. Any last thoughts before we wrap up?

Julian:
I just… what you said in my introduction about we can all achieve success together, and I want people to take this on board and get the success that they deserve and help other people do the same.

Ivan:
Well, thank you. We’re gonna have the sample checklist on this podcast, so please take a look at it. And Julian, thank you so much for helping co-write Infinite Giving and Infinite Legacy, which is our new book together. I appreciate you.

Julian:
My pleasure.

Ivan:
Priscilla, over to you.

Priscilla:
Okay, great. Thank you both so much for the great information.

This podcast is sponsored by www.misneraudioprograms.com. These audio programs will provide you with the tools and the inspiration to powerfully enhance your BNI experience. So check out the great material available to you at www.misneraudioprograms.com and then use the promo code IVAN50 for 50% off of everything [this code is good for a limited time]. All of the proceeds go to the BNI Foundation.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

title.rendered: Episode 880: Giving Is Transformational (Classic)

content.rendered:
Note: This is a rebroadcast of Episode 691, first published in January 2021. Links and special offers may have expired.

Synopsis

This week Dr. Misner focuses on the idea that giving is transformational. This topic comes from the book Infinite Giving, which Dr. Misner wrote with Greg Davies and Julian Lewis.

Here are some of the way that giving transforms us, according to studies:

  • When people give to others, it activates regions of the brain associated with pleasure, social connection and trust, creating a “warm glow” effect.
  • People who volunteered to multiple organizations were 44% less likely to die over a five-year period than non-volunteers.
  • The more extensive the reciprocal altruism born of a social connection, the greater the advance towards health, wealth, and happiness.

Consciously giving has a profound ripple effect, as BNI members around the world demonstrated in 2020. The beginning of the year is a great time to be a role model for the people around you. Start the year by really being there to support and help your fellow BNI members and others. You can start small, but start today.

Complete Transcript of Episode 691

Priscilla:

Hello everybody and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio in Berkeley, California. And I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan. How are you? And where are you?

Ivan:
Oh, Priscilla, I have been to so many countries, online, over the last few weeks, I can’t even keep count anymore. I mean, one of the advantages of doing so many meetings online is that you can be in one part of the world in the morning and a completely different part of the world in the afternoon. So, that part has been really fantastic. So visiting a lot of regions around the world.

Priscilla:

That’s really amazing. I think that’s great. So tell us what you’re offering us today. What do you have?

Ivan:

Yeah, so my topic today is Giving is Transformational. The material, the concept, is from a book that I’ve talked about here on BNI podcast, and I’ve had my co-authors on, the book is Infinite Giving. And I co-wrote it with Greg Davies and Julian Lewis, it’s a great little book, and you’ll see it on the side here of BNI podcast. If you have a chance, pick up a copy. It’s a quick read. And so, I want to talk about the transformational aspect of giving. You know, the world can be a pretty dark and contentious place. We’ve got illness, famine, poverty, hostility, there’s desperation all around us. But it doesn’t have to be that way for us. The world can be a better place, and we can make a choice, a choice to be a voice of change. And that change can transform the world we live in. Business people, I believe, are well positioned to help make that change.

Years ago, I implemented the phrase into the fabric of the core values of the company, that simple phrase is Givers Gain, and it was really a game changer in giving people a different framework relating to the way they network with others. You know, Givers Gain is so integrated into the DNA of BNI, we, I think, sometimes forget that this was a completely different strategy in 1985. I mean, to most people networking was about hunting, it was about, you know, finding someone and closing deals. And the idea of Givers Gain was truly a transformational idea.

And I love the way that it has been so incredibly embraced throughout the organization. I think that giving is an idea that proclaims that we can be something bigger than ourselves. It’s a reaffirmation that our lives have significant meaning and that through community we can be our best selves. For people who get that, this philosophy, I think, unlocks a whole new world of meaning and opportunity. So our job in BNI, I believe, is to help people get it. More importantly, we can be intentional about helping people get it to reach that place.

In 2010, Greater Good magazine wrote that the National Institutes of Health found that when people give to others, it activates regions of the brain associated with pleasure, social connection and trust, creating a warm glow effect, they called it. Scientists also believe that altruistic behavior releases endorphins in the brain producing the positive feeling known as the helpers high. Researchers at the University of California Berkeley, your neck of the woods, Priscilla, found that people who volunteered to multiple organizations were 44% less likely to die over a five-year period than non-volunteers. That blew me away. The author of Loneliness: Human Nature and the Need for Social Connection, says that the more extensive the reciprocal altruism born of a social connection, the greater the advance towards health, wealth, and happiness. And I think that’s really the foundation of what BNI is, and certainly should be, around the world. So there is, I think, a profound ripple effect that takes place through the act of consciously practicing a giving philosophy. And I’ve seen the dramatic impact that one person can have on another, never any more than last year. The 2020 year, I was just so proud of BNI and BNI members, and what they did for each other.

I’ve observed the surging ripple that takes place as an act impacts one person, and then the next person and the next person and so on. Within a community or a network, that ripple effect that takes place from this philosophy can influence hundreds, and it can influence thousands of people. In fact, I really believe we can influence the world. That’s why our vision statement is Changing the Way the World Does Business. I think I’ve said this in a previous podcast, but it’s worth repeating: Giving is contagious. Conscious giving can be infinite. There’s a Chinese saying, ”If you want happiness for an hour, take a nap. If you want happiness for a day, go fishing. If you want happiness for a year, inherit a fortune. If you want to happiness for a lifetime, help somebody.”

For centuries, I think, the greatest thinkers have suggested the same thing. Happiness is found in giving and helping others. The results from this philosophy cannot be observed as a straight line in life. It’s a complex zigzag of interconnected relationships that lead to a place of contentment and fulfillment, and it begins with the right mindset. It’s about taking off your bib and putting on an apron. It’s not about what’s in it for you, or how much you can get. It’s about serving and helping others in a very conscious way. Not doing so in a way that ignores the fact that there are takers in the world. But in a way that consciously chooses to find your fellow givers in the world. It’s easy to be cynical, and to think that the help you can offer is negligible. But it only takes a small gesture to, sometimes, change people’s lives.

I’ve talked about this in podcasts before and I think it’s so important. We’ve all had people who are in our story. They’re people that we talk about who have changed our life in some way. However, I think something’s even more important. To me, the real question is not who’s in your story, but whose story are you in? Whose life have we made a difference in? This is what creates a meaningful life. We are in the beginning of the year; it’s a great time to be a role model for the people around you. To start the year really being there to support and help your fellow BNI members and others.

We often, I think, look at the world and hope that someday things will be different. But I have a good friend, as matter of fact, I just had dinner with her a few nights ago, Sam Horn, and Sam says something that I really love. She even has a book by this name: Someday Is Not a Day of the Week. I love that expression. Someday is not a day of the week. If we wait for someday, we’re waiting for others to make the first move. I believe someday can be today. And it can start with us. Today is as good a day as any to be our Someday. We can all make a difference in people’s lives starting today. And that difference starts with a transformational change within us, and how we deal with people around us, and the world beyond them.

We would be giving without a transactional expectation, giving to people in all parts of our life, appropriately. We would understand that we give what we can afford and that it’s okay to gain. Give what you can afford, give from your saucer, not your cup; it’s a phrase that we use in Infinite Giving. And that it’s absolutely okay to gain in such an environment. I think it’s important that we remain humble, where saying thank you is the norm. And I think that we can start small. But we can start today. Begin local but think global. If we influence one life, you begin to influence the world. So just start with that person next to you. And know that you may not make a world of difference, but you can make a difference in the world. And you do that one person at a time. Priscilla, I thought this was an important message to start off the new year after having such a challenging year last year. It’s a great message to begin this year with.

Priscilla:
I totally agree, Ivan. I think it’s beautiful. I don’t know how to state this exactly. But giving is something that gives me huge pleasure. So I love this podcast.

Ivan:
Yeah, thank you. And I would urge people to take a look at the book, Infinite Giving. It’s a quick read, you can read it in two hours. We talk about so many things in there, about what the 7 Principles are for Givers Gain, and that, you know, being an infinite giver doesn’t mean you’re an infinite victim. There are lines, you have to have discernment. We talk about that. But it has to start from a position of being willing to give and to help and support other people. And if you start with that in mind, and have discernment as you go through life and through your business, I think you’ll find, you know, that at the end of your career you’ll look back and really feel like you’ve made a difference in people’s lives. And more importantly, they’ll look at you and feel like you’ve made a difference in their lives. And I think that’s a life well lived.

Priscilla:
Mm-hmm, me too.

Ivan:
So that’s it for today, Priscilla. I hope you enjoyed that message.

Priscilla:
I totally enjoyed that message. Thank you so much. It was wonderful.

Ivan:
Thank you.

All of the proceeds go to the BNI Foundation.

title.rendered: Episode 879: It’s a Small World After All

content.rendered:
Synopsis

What are the odds of connecting with another BNI member by accident, just by dialing a number incorrectly? That exact thing happened to Dr. Misner when he was first in the process of moving to Texas. He tried dialing a California number with his new Texas mobile and forgot to enter the area code. The person in Austin who picked up the phone just happened to be a BNI member…who had met Dr. Misner at a training a few months previously.

Recognizing a great opportunity, the BNI member scheduled a One-to-One with Dr. Misner, and they ended up doing business together.

Have you connected with someone accidentally and turned that connection into business? Share your stories about interesting, unusual, and surprising connections in the comments.

Complete Transcript of Episode 879

Priscilla:
Hello everybody, and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak recording studio in Berkeley, California, and I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan, how are you and where are you?

Ivan:
I am still in Austin. However, I’m headed soon to the Caribbean for a BNI Cruise for a membership drive in the United States. We have a huge, huge number of members who won the cruise, and I’ll be in the Caribbean for a few days, and then in Miami [Florida] for a BNI event.

Priscilla:
Okay, that sounds great.

Ivan:
Yeah.

Priscilla:
So what’s this podcast?

Ivan:
Yeah, so the title sounds a little bit like a Disney song, right Priscilla?

Priscilla:
Oh my gosh.

Ivan:
And I know you love this song. So would you like to sing it?

Priscilla: (laughing)
I hate this actual ride in Disneyland. I got stuck on it once, and I heard that song so many times I wanted to, you know, whatever.

Ivan:
You know, kids love it, that’s for sure. But it is kind of a true statement. It is a small world. Even decades after founding and running BNI, I’m still impressed at the fact that the world can be so small sometimes. So I’m going to share a fun story from a few years ago, and I’m changing the names for privacy, but it’s an absolute true story.

I had done a BNI Leadership Team training here in Austin, even though I was kind of living between LA [Los Angeles] and Austin at the time, and the training really went well. I met lots of fantastic members and directors, and a few months later, I was back in the LA area at my California office. At the time, I had a house for sale in the LA community of Claremont. And there was a problem. I had a neighbor who was burglarized, and my house had a security camera outside, which hopefully, and in fact did, may have recorded some helpful information for the police.

So I got a phone call from the Claremont Police Department, but I couldn’t take the call when it as it came in, so my assistant gave me the message and the phone number. Well, I had just recently, because I was transitioning from California to Austin, I had recently gotten a new mobile phone number with an Austin, Texas area code. And without thinking, I picked up my mobile phone and I dialed the number for the Claremont Police Department in California to return their call. The thing is, I didn’t put in the area code for California. I just dialed the number so… and for Austin, the area code is 512, and California was 909.

So I inadvertently dialed the number with a 512 area code, because I didn’t put in the area code. So someone answered the phone, and I said, “Hi. This is Ivan Misner.” And I thought, wait a minute. He said, Hello. He didn’t say Claremont police. I said, “I’m sorry. Is this the Claremont Police Department?” And then he said, “Ivan?”

Priscilla: (laughing)

Ivan:
“Ivan Misner? Dr. Misner with BNI?” And I’m like, “Yeah, I’m guessing this isn’t the Claremont Police Department.” He said, “No, this is Frank Davis. I’m a BNI member in Austin.”

Priscilla: (laughing) 
That’s great.

Ivan:
Okay, there are over a million people in the Austin area. What are the chances that I would have connected with one of the hundreds and hundreds of BNI members out of the million people in the Austin area? And yet, I had indeed called a BNI member because I didn’t use the Claremont area code, and I had a new Austin telephone number. So I got him, and it turned out that I had just happened to have met him a few weeks earlier.

Priscilla:
Oh, wow, that’s so funny.

Ivan:
Because he was at the training.

Priscilla:
Oh, wow.

Ivan:
And he told me… actually it was a few months. He said, “We met a few months ago at the Leadership Team Training that you did.” And I thought, oh my goodness, this is amazing. And then, of course, I had to explain to him why I was calling him and why I thought he was the Claremont, California Police Department, and he laughed. He happened to be an attorney, and he laughed, and he thought it was funny. And I’ve got to say it was funny also because he turned it into an inspired BNI One-to-One meeting idea.

Priscilla:
Uh huh.

Ivan:
He said, “Gosh, this would be really great to have a One-to-One with you. And you know, since you called me and let’s talk and let’s meet.” And how could I possibly say no, right? I mean, with hundreds of thousands of members, the only time I do One-to-Ones now is on Founders Day, which is the last Thursday of June every year. And I do a whole day of One-to-Ones with BNI members. But I thought, geesh, I can’t say no to this guy. I just interrupted his day and thought he was the police department.

And so we did a One-to-One and because of that conversation, we connected, and I actually ended up doing business with him in his legal practice. I think this is a great story on multiple levels. One is, it truly is a small world. What are the statistical chances of me connecting with somebody who was part of my organization by accident, simply because I called a number that happened to be a different telephone area code and got him?

Priscilla:
Right.

Ivan:
And what are the chances that I would have just met him a few months earlier? I mean…

Priscilla:
Yeah.

Ivan:
You know, that’s like movie fodder, right? That’s like something that would happen in a movie as something strange. It’s also, I think, a story about recognizing an opportunity when it arises and taking action. So Frank jumped on the opportunity with the quick thinking of asking for a meeting, and I was more than willing to do that under the circumstances. So here’s another terrific aspect of the story. It was a really good One-to-One.

I enjoyed talking with him, and we found some overlapping areas of interest, in self development training. I referred him over to Jack Canfield, who’s a long-time friend, and most of you know, I married Jody, who works for Jack. I didn’t know Jody at the time, but years later, I married Jody and met her working for Jack Canfield. And so I referred him to a Jack Canfield training. I should double check; I bet she sold the training to him. I never thought to ask her. I’m going to ask her if she sold the training to him, because I know he called.

Anyway, he expressed a lot of interest in the BNI Foundation, and I was also able to connect him with the right person for more information on the Foundation. And I ended up doing some business with him. I think it’s a win-win-win story. It’s a fantastic example of you just never know how you may connect with people, and then take the opportunity to do something when you do make an unexpected connection. So to him, I say, well done. I’m sorry I called you. I didn’t mean to bother you, but you jumped on that opportunity, and it was a pleasure spending some time with you.

And I think just to wrap up, if you’ve got any thoughts, I’m happy to hear them. But just to wrap up — this story about accidentally connecting with a BNI member in another state because of the wrong phone number, shows how close the degrees of separation can be. The unexpected result reinforces the message that it really, truly is a small world, after all, and that message can inspire us to be open to the possibilities of new connections in our life. I’d love for you, if you’re listening to this podcast or reading the transcript, I’d love for you to share your stories about interestingly unusual and surprising connections that you may have made in your lifetime. Please put them here on BNI Podcast. Any last thoughts?

Priscilla:
Yeah, I just wanted to say that it might be a pretty good example of synchronistic energy that happened. I mean, you have to be a kind of a believer in that concept that things happen for a reason.

Ivan:
Yeah, well, the older I get, the more I believe in that kind of synchronicity. And, you know, and to some extent, paying attention, because he was in the middle of something when I called him. He could have easily said, “Look, you got the wrong number.” and hung up without thinking about the name I just gave him. And so, you know, he was paying attention. I accidentally called him. There is a synchronicity in all of that taking place.

Priscilla:
Yeah, yeah.

Ivan:
And I ended up doing business for a number of years as a result of that accidental phone call.

Priscilla:
Well, I want to just also say one more thing, which is that you told me before we started recording today that there are now 328,000 members in BNI as of today.

Ivan:
Yeah, as of today’s date, there are over 328,000 members in more than 75 countries around the world.

Priscilla:
That is impressive.

Ivan:
That is humbling to me as to what this has become.

Priscilla: Yeah. So you found one of them. (chuckling)

Ivan:
I did. I accidentally of the hundreds of millions and billions of people in the world that I could have called. I happened to accidentally call a BNI member, which I think is hysterical.

Priscilla:
That is so funny. Okay, well, that’s great, Ivan.

Ivan:
Thank you, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Thank you so much. That’s a really fun podcast.

This podcast is sponsored by www.misneraudioprograms.com. These audio programs will provide you with the tools and the inspiration to powerfully enhance your BNI experience. So check out the great material available to you at www.misneraudioprograms.com and then use the promo code IVAN50 for 50% off of everything [this code is good for a limited time]. All of the proceeds go to the BNI Foundation.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.
